questions.questionText: Which part of the plant absorbs water from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil. They also help anchor the plant in the ground.
hints: It helps the plant stay in one place

questions.questionText: What do plants need to make their food?
questions.explanation: Plants make their food through photosynthesis using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide from the air.
hints: Think about what green leaves need

questions.questionText: Which part of the plant makes seeds?
questions.explanation: Flowers are the reproductive part of plants. After pollination, flowers develop into fruits that contain seeds.
hints: This is the colorful part of many plants
Bees and butterflies visit this part

questions.questionText: What is the green color in leaves called?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in leaves that helps plants make food using sunlight.

questions.questionText: Which type of plant has a soft, green stem?
questions.explanation: Herbs are small plants with soft, green stems. Examples include mint, coriander, and basil.
hints: They usually live for one season

questions.questionText: What gas do plants take in during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide from the air during photosynthesis and release oxygen.
hints: This is the gas we breathe out
Plants use this to make food

questions.questionText: Which of these is NOT a part of a plant?
questions.explanation: Plants do not have a brain. The main parts of a plant are roots, stem, leaves, flowers, and fruits.
hints: Plants don't have organs like animals

questions.questionText: What do we call plants that grow in water?
questions.explanation: Aquatic plants are plants that live in water. Examples include lotus, water lily, and hydrilla.
